evals.prompt: Can you check if TodayView is accessible? I want to make sure VoiceOver works properly and the touch targets are big enough
evals.expected_output: Agent should read TodayView and its subviews, check VoiceOver labels/hints/traits, verify touch targets are >= 44pt, and check Dynamic Type support
expectations: The agent reads TodayView.swift and key subviews (like TaskRowView)
The agent checks for accessibility labels on interactive elements
The agent checks for accessibility traits (.isHeader, .isButton)
The agent verifies touch targets are >= 44pt
The agent checks that DesignSystem.Typography tokens are used (not hardcoded fonts)
The agent provides specific SwiftUI modifier fixes for any issues found

evals.prompt: run a full accessibility audit — we got a review saying the app is hard to use with VoiceOver. Check everything please
evals.expected_output: Agent should audit all major views for VoiceOver, Dynamic Type, color contrast, and touch targets, producing a comprehensive report
expectations: The agent audits multiple views, not just one screen
The agent checks color contrast ratios against WCAG AA standards
The agent checks that information isn't conveyed by color alone
The agent checks both light and dark mode color combinations
The agent produces a structured report organized by issue category
The agent suggests accessibility snapshot tests for views with issues
